encounter.chiefComplaint: Blood pressure follow-up; occasional exertional chest tightness

clinicalNote.subjective: Patient presents for blood pressure follow-up. Reports occasional exertional chest tightness. Home BP monitoring ongoing. No chest pain at rest, no syncope, no dyspnea at rest.
clinicalNote.objective: BP 128/80 mmHg, HR 72 bpm, RR 16, Temp 36.8°C, SpO2 98%. Weight 84.5 kg, Height 175 cm. Cardiovascular exam unremarkable. Lungs clear.

assessment.problem: Exertional chest discomfort — low clinical suspicion for ACS; exertional component warrants structured cardiovascular risk review

clinicalNote.plan: Continue lisinopril 10 mg PO daily; reinforce lifestyle modification. Order lipid panel, fasting glucose or HbA1c, BMP; schedule exercise tolerance testing or cardiology referral if symptoms persist or worsen. RTC 8 weeks with home BP log (twice daily x 7 days before visit).
clinicalNote.patientInstructions: Seek emergency care for prolonged/rest chest pain, severe shortness of breath, or fainting. Bring medication list and home BP readings to next visit.

agentContext.oneLineSummary: 32M with controlled-stage hypertension on ACE-I reporting rare exertional chest tightness; CV risk workup planned.
keyFactsForTools: ACE inhibitor therapy active — watch hyperkalemia/renal function with BMP.
Exertional chest symptoms present — exclude ACS red flags; document tolerance and triggers.
Home BP monitoring requested — aggregate averages for medication titration decisions.
